message:
fix(clippy): pedantic and nursery lint compliance across core engine crates

Systematic remediation of clippy pedantic and nursery warnings across 12
core library crates. Changes are mechanical and behavior-preserving — no
logic changes, only lint fixes and targeted suppressions.

Self-referential type patterns (fsqlite-btree):
- Replace BeMessage::Insert/Delete with Self::Insert/Self::Delete
- Replace BeNode::Leaf with Self::Leaf, Vec<BeNode<K,V>> with Vec<Self>
- Replace DeltaKind array type with [Self; 3] and BindingState with [Self; 3]

Numeric precision and casting:
- Replace `n as f64` with `f64::from(n)` for lossless i32→f64 conversion
  in differential_privacy test statistics (mean/variance calculations)
- Replace `u as u64` with `u64::from(b)` for byte→u64 widening in
  flat_combining hash function
- Use .mul_add() instead of manual multiply-add in learned_index segment
  prediction and differential_privacy Laplace sampling for FMA precision
- Use .abs_diff() instead of manual if/else absolute difference in
  learned_index lookup error calculation (3 call sites)
- Use .saturating_add(1) instead of .checked_add(1).unwrap_or(u64::MAX)
  in fault_vfs jitter span calculation

Debug formatting:
- Add .finish_non_exhaustive() instead of .finish() on 8 manual Debug
  impls that omit fields (DpEngine, FlatCombiner, LeftRight, LeftRightPair,
  BeTree, CoolingStateMachine, CrackedColumn, plus their annotated
  #[allow(clippy::missing_fields_in_debug)])

Pattern matching:
- Replace match-Some/None with let...else in learned_index find_segment
- Merge PropagationResult::Success and ::Partial arms with shared
  widgets_invalidated field using | pattern
- Replace if-negative with if-positive condition first in cooling.rs
  resolve_frame (Cold→None else Some)
- Use (0.05..=0.07).contains(&discount) instead of manual range check
  in vectorized benchmark
- Sort-unstable for primitive types in cracking tests

Targeted suppressions for legitimate patterns:
- #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] on finalize_prepared_concurrent_commit
  (single complex transaction finalization), execute_insert_select_fallback
  (fallback path with extensive error handling), verify_metrics_contract
  (exhaustive contract checking), check_write (fault injection state machine)
- #[allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening)] on alloc_commit_seq,
  gc_tick, load_page, metrics_snapshot (lock guards intentionally held
  across multiple operations for atomicity)
- #[allow(clippy::self_only_used_in_recursion)] on BeTree::get_in_node,
  collect_range, collect_all (recursive tree traversal methods)
- #[allow(clippy::unused_self)] on validate_params, apply_latency
  (method receivers for future state access)
- #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation/wrap/sign_loss)] at specific
  cast sites in connection.rs (affected row count i64), learned_index
  (position prediction), differential_privacy (epsilon micros), and
  flat_combining (elapsed nanoseconds)
- #[allow(dead_code)] on learned_index Segment.pos_lo field (retained
  for debugging/future use) and harness test helpers

Additional fixes:
- Add explicit drop(arena) in gc_tick before acquiring visibility_ranges
  write lock to prevent potential deadlock from holding both locks
- Add explicit drop(pages) in cooling.rs temperature_counts and
  cooldown_scan to release mutex before returning
- Derive Eq alongside PartialEq on PropagationConfig and
  PropagationMetrics (bloodstream structs with no float fields)
- Add `use std::fmt::Write as _` import where write! macro is used on
  String buffers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
